messages.content:
Through market research it was determined the following position were required for tree removal and stump grounding.

•	Project Manager
•	Certified Arborist
•	Tree Climber
•	Ground Crew
•	Stump Grinder

Based on the department of Labor’s wage data for Fort Bragg NC. The following was determined to suffice work efficiently.
•	Project Manager: 1 full-time employee. 40 hours/week. $35.29/hour
•	Certified Arborist: 1 full-time employee. 40 hours/week. $25.68/hour
•	Tree Climber: 2 full-time employees. 40 hours/week per employee.  $20.60/hour
•	Ground Crew: 2 full-time employees. 40 hours/week per employee. $15.92/hour
•	Stump Grinder: 1 full-time employee. 40 hours/week. $19.00/hour

*This does not include any additional costs such as equipment rental or disposal fees.

Factors to consider include: 
•	The scope is projected for 50 work orders per year.

•	Tree size and location - The size and location of the tree affect the cost of its removal. A larger tree or one located in a difficult-to-reach area will require more equipment and labor, thus increasing the overall cost.

•	Species of tree - Different tree species have different densities, which impact the time and effort required to remove them. Certain species may also require special equipment, increasing the cost of removal.

•	Stump size and location - Similarly to trees, stump size and location play a significant role in determining the cost. A larger stump or one located in a hard-to-reach spot will require additional effort and equipment, leading to higher costs.

•	Access to the site - The accessibility of the work site also influences costs, as equipment and labor may need to navigate obstacles such as buildings, fences, or uneven terrain, and the cost for this may vary. 

•	Safety considerations - The safety of the workers and the site must be taken into consideration. Depending on the nature of the job or location, additional safety equipment or protocols could be necessary, which can add to the overall cost of the project. 

•	Additional services - If additional services are required or requested, such as hauling away debris or grinding the stump, they will also increase the cost of removal.

By taking these factors into account we can prepare a more accurate and comprehensive independent cost estimate for tree removal and stump grinding in Fort Bragg. knowing all this information I need to know the annual hours per employee projected for 50 jobs per year

Based on the projection of 50 work orders per year, each full-time employee will work approximately 2,000 hours per year (50 weeks x 40 hours per week). Therefore, the breakdown of annual hours per employee for this tree removal and stump grinding project is as follows:

• Project Manager: 2,000 hours per year
• Certified Arborist: 2,000 hours per year
• Tree Climber (x2): 4,000 hours per year (2,000 hours per employee)
• Ground Crew (x2): 4,000 hours per year (2,000 hours per employee)
• Stump Grinder: 2,000 hours per year

It's important to note that these projections may vary depending on the complexity of each job and the time required to complete it, but this provides a rough estimate for annual hours per employee for the given workload.
based on the information how many full time employee would I need

According to the information provided, you would need a total of 7 full-time employees for this tree removal and stump grinding project. The breakdown of positions and required number of employees is as follows:

• Project Manager: 1 full-time employee
• Certified Arborist: 1 full-time employee
• Tree Climber: 2 full-time employees
• Ground Crew: 2 full-time employees
• Stump Grinder: 1 full-time employee

Keep in mind, however, that these numbers are based on the assumption of a workload of 50 work orders per year with an estimated number of work hours per employee. The actual number of employees needed could vary depending on the actual workload and other factors.

I made a list of tools and equipment required but I dont know how many of each would I need.  see list Stump Grinder
chainsaw 
harnesses
climbing ropes
ladders
Truck mounted Crane
bobcat/excavator
dump truck
axe/machete
saws
rachets/tie-down straps
trailer
backpack blower
hedge trimmers
hand pruners/shears
pry bars 
shovels 
rakes 
wheelbarrows
fuel tanks
wood chipper

The number of tools and equipment required will vary based on the number of crews and the size and complexity of each job. However, assuming one crew with the appropriate number of personnel, the following quantities could be useful:

• Stump Grinder - 1 machine
• Chainsaw - 2 or more
• Harnesses - 5 or more depending on the number of climbers
• Climbing ropes - 5 or more depending on the number of climbers
• Ladders - 2 or more
• Truck mounted crane - 1 machine 
• Bobcat/excavator - 1 machine
• Dump truck - 1 truck or more
• Axe/machete - 2 or more
• Saws - 2 or more
• Ratchets/Tie-down straps - 4 or more
• Trailer - 1 trailer or more
• Backpack blower - 1 or more
• Hedge trimmers - 2 or more
• Hand pruners/shears - 2 or more
• Pry bars - 2 or more
• Shovels - 2 or more
• Rakes - 2 or more
• Wheelbarrows - 2 or more
• Fuel tanks - 2 or more
• Wood chipper - 1 machine

Again, the actual number of tools and equipment required will depend on the specific needs of the job and the number of crew members working.
what would be the price for each unit mention aboved

The cost of each unit mentioned will depend on the specific brand, quality, and type of equipment. Here are some rough estimates based on common prices for good quality equipment:

• Stump Grinder - $2,000 to $10,000+
• Chainsaw - $150 to $500+
• Harnesses - $50 to $200+ each depending on safety rating
• Climbing ropes - $150 to $300+ per rope depending on length and quality
• Ladders - $100 to $300+ depending on length and durability
• Truck mounted crane - $15,000 to $50,000+
• Bobcat/excavator - $30,000 to $70,000+
• Dump truck - $20,000 to $50,000+
• Axe/machete - $20 to $50+
• Saws - $50 to $500+ depending on type and quality
• Ratchets/Tie-down straps - $10 to $30+ each depending on quality and length
• Trailer - $2,000 to $5,000+ depending on size and quality
• Backpack blower - $150 to $400+ depending on brand and power
• Hedge trimmers - $50 to $200+ depending on brand and quality
• Hand pruners/shears - $20 to $50+ depending on brand and quality
• Pry bars - $20 to $50+ depending on size and quality
• Shovels - $20 to $100+ depending on size and quality
• Rakes - $20 to $50+ depending on size and quality
• Wheelbarrows - $50 to $200+ depending on size and quality
• Fuel tanks - $20 to $200+ depending on size and quality
• Wood chipper - $1,500 to $10,000+ depending on brand and size

These are just estimates and the actual prices may vary depending on the brand, type, and quality of the equipment. It's important to do research and compare prices to find the best deals and ensure that the equipment meets safety standards and is suitable for the job.
can you give me a better estimated knowing that the goverment is providing the tools and equipment.

If the government is providing the equipment and tools, then the costs will be significantly less for you. However, it's important to note that the government may have certain regulations or requirements on the type of equipment and tools that can be used for the job.

I need a more exact estimate.  since the government fall under more strict guidelines of safety

I apologize, but without knowing the specific guidelines and regulations set by the government on safety equipment, it is difficult to give an exact estimate. However, if the government is providing the tools and equipment, they will be consistent with safety regulations and standards.

It's important to note that safety equipment requirements can vary depending on the job site and nature of the work being performed. Some safety equipment that may be required by the government could include:

• Hard hats
• High visibility safety vests or shirts
• Eye protection (such as safety goggles or glasses)
• Hearing protection (such as earplugs or earmuffs)
• Respiratory protection (such as masks or respirators)
• Gloves
• Steel-toed boots or safety shoes
• Safety harnesses
• Fall protection systems (such as safety nets or guardrails)

The costs of safety equipment will depend on the specific requirements and regulations set forth by the government. Some of these items may be provided by the government, while others may need to be purchased. In any case, it's important to make sure that all safety equipment meets the necessary standards and regulations.
